items.title: MSG Suing Wired Over Article Alleging Company Kept Database Of LGBTQIA Celebrities
items.summary: A week ago, Wired published a report alleging Madison Square Garden Entertainment Corp. maintains a database of LGBTQIA celebrities. At the time, MSG said they'd be suing Wired over the article. As promised, that lawsuit was filed today. The post MSG Suing <em>Wired</em> Over Article Alleging Company Kept Database Of LGBTQIA Celebrities appeared first on Stereogum.
items.audio: audio/MSG Suing Wired Over Article Alleging Company Kept.mp3

items.title: Robert Smith Does Not Have World Cup Halftime Show Fever
items.summary: This year's FIFA Men's World Cup took place in the United States, Canada, and Mexico, and it's been Americanized to an unprecedented extent. No, I'm not talking about Donald Trump's meddling with Folarin Balogun's red card suspension; FIFA was already quite corrupt before our corrupt president got involved. But I am referring to the hydration breaks during each half that somehow became ad breaks.
items.audio: audio/Robert Smith Does Not Have World Cup Halftime Show.mp3

items.title: Hana Stretton – “Seagull Theory II” & “As It Was Before This”
items.summary: The British-Australian musical artist Hana Stretton specializes in ambient folk, seemingly merging samples, field recordings, and original tracks into soft smears of emotion. Her songs inspire warm comparisons to geniuses like Grouper and Claire Rousay, and she's gained a fervent supporter in Phil Elverum, who reissued her 2023 album Soon on his label P.W. Elverum & Suns.
items.audio: audio/Hana Stretton _ _Seagull Theory II_ _ _As It Was B.mp3
